threats.name: third_party_origin
threats.description: Nested repo points at a third-party GitHub remote, so upstream trust and update provenance are external to Project_ARIS.

threats.name: detached_snapshot
threats.description: The working tree is checked out at a detached HEAD commit older than the local main branch tip, which makes the snapshot easy to drift from the maintained branch.

threats.name: shadow_dependency
threats.description: The snapshot is referenced by 31 governance/test files in Project_ARIS, so removal would break read-only audit flows even though runtime does not consume it.

threats.name: transitive_model_download
threats.description: The nested project downloads a Hugging Face model at runtime, introducing an external dependency even though the server itself is read-only.

mitigations: Keep the snapshot quarantined under external/mcp_candidates.
Do not promote to product/runtime dependency.
Avoid removal until the governance/test references are retired or repointed.
If integration is ever desired, convert explicitly to submodule or vendor it with a pinned policy.
